_meta.source: shipbuilder/tools/game_logic_notes.md, Finding 13 (Rockwood Nut), Finding 14 (Spacekorn), and Finding 16 (continuous gate-checking/death-timer/Invasive-spread mechanics) - decompiled from data.cdb sheet 'farm' and ent.b.PlotZone.pickVariant/hasMinRequirement/updatePlots/checkProgress (src/ent/b/Farm.hx). Hand-transcribed, not script-regenerated - if game_logic_notes.md gets a new/corrected finding for these crops, update this file to match.
_meta.mechanism: The Xenic Farm building has one shared Temperature dial (Cold/Temperate/Warm/Hot) and one shared Light dial (UV/Natural/Dark) - a single farm is never in two temperature or light states at once. Each grown variant's gate is a set of acceptable dial positions (OR-across-allowed-states); a variant with an empty temperature/light list is unconstrained (any dial position satisfies it) - EXCEPT a variant marked 'unreachable: true' (see that field's own note below), whose empty list means the exact opposite. After germination, ALL variants whose gates currently pass become candidates and the game picks uniformly at random among them (not priority order) - if none pass, the plant dies instead of stalling.
_meta.growth_death_mechanism: Requirements are checked continuously, not just once - letting fertilizer, dial position, or a neighbor restriction go unmet stalls growth and starts a 5-hour death countdown. Restoring it in time is fully recoverable. Once a plant reaches its final harvest stage, requirements stop mattering entirely.
_meta.enrichment_trigger: An enrichment entry's optional 'trigger' field ({kind: 'temp'|'light'|'neighbor_tag', values: [...]}) marks the ones frontend/js/farming.js can render as a pill instead of plain text: 'temp'/'light' are gated by the SAME shared Temperature/Light dial as the variant's own grow-gate, rendered as the same dial chips used in Requirements; 'neighbor_tag' names a bio-tag (see each variant's own 'bio_tag' and 'neighbor_restriction_tag') a neighbor plant must carry, rendered as the same pill used for the bio-tag badge and the Requirements Neighbor line. Fertilizer conditions carry no trigger - there's no established pill style for those.
_meta.fertilizer_forbidden_any: A variant's optional 'fertilizer_forbidden_any: true' means NO fertilizer/supplement of any kind may be present - genuinely different from an empty 'fertilizer_required' (which just means no SPECIFIC fertilizer is required, any or none is fine). Only Rockwood Glow (Glowwood) is marked this way, per live in-game observation - Finding 13's own table wrote its Fertilizer-required cell as the distinct word 'none' (vs the dash '-' every unconstrained variant elsewhere in Findings 13/14 uses), consistent with this, but this specific reading (unlike the 'unreachable' field below) has NOT been re-checked against raw disassembly - flag for follow-up if that's ever done.
_meta.unreachable: A variant's optional 'unreachable: true' (+ 'unreachable_note', the player-facing explanation frontend/js/farming.js shows in a warning banner) means its grow-gate can NEVER pass in normal play, confirmed by both raw disassembly and a direct in-game test - it is not just rare or hard, it's a dead branch of pickVariant. Only Rockwood Glow (Glowwood) is marked this way: its 'temperature'/'light' fields are present with a literal integer 0 in the source data rather than being absent, and hasMinRequirement only skips the dial check on an ABSENT field - a present 0 always fails the bitwise-AND test, every tick, no matter the farm's dial position. This is the opposite of every other 'unconstrained' variant in Findings 13/14 (which all use an absent key), so Glowwood's empty temperature/light arrays here do NOT mean 'any dial position works' the way they do for other variants - see this field's own presence as the disambiguator, and always check it before treating an empty temperature/light list as unconstrained. A theorized loophole (stacking Dreamwood neighbors' allowMissing tolerance to cover both misses) was investigated and ruled out - it caps at 1, Glow needs 2. The only way to place it at all is a debug/admin-only function, not normal play.
_meta.speed_effect: An enrichment's optional 'speed_effect' ([{stat: 'growth'|'fruit'|'byproduct', type: 'additive'|'multiplicative', value}]) is ONLY present when that enrichment actually changes a cycle TIME (growth_hours/fruit_cycle_hours/byproduct_cycle_hours) - several enrichments instead boost output QUANTITY (e.g. 'Byproduct quantity +100%'), which this app doesn't track at all (no yield/quantity field exists anywhere in this data), so those carry no speed_effect and stay plain, non-interactive text in frontend/js/farming.js. 'additive' value 0.5 means +50% speed (duration divided by 1.5); 'multiplicative' value 0.6 means the speed itself is multiplied by 0.6 (duration divided by 0.6, i.e. SLOWER despite being an enrichment 'bonus' - Finding 13's own ARatio/MRatio semantics, applied literally). A variant's own 'neighbor_effects' ([{label, speed_effect, dial_group?}]) are the same shape, sourced from a DIFFERENT variant's adjacency entry rather than this variant's own enrichments - currently just two real cases: Spacekorn Plain's self-buff from a neighboring Spacekorn Plain, and Rockwood Glow's 'neighbor treated as UV-lit' effect (mirrors the receiving variant's own Light=UV enrichment numbers, so it's grouped into that variant's own 'light' dial_group - being UV-lit via a Glow neighbor and via the farm's actual Light dial are the same state, not two stacking bonuses). Entries (both enrichments and neighbor_effects) that share a 'dial_group' (temp/light) are mutually exclusive in the UI (radio-button-style) since the farm only has one Temperature and one Light position at a time - entries with no dial_group (fertilizer- or neighbor-tag-gated, or Plain's own neighbor buff) toggle independently, since a plot can hold multiple simultaneous supplements/conditions.

variants.name: Rockwood Green
adjacency: Neighbor seed germination speed +100%; neighbor seed death speed ×0.5

enrichments.effect: Growth speed / Liquid consumption / Supplement consumption all ×0.6 (-40% each)

variants.name: Rockwood Dream
adjacency: Neighbor tolerates 1 unmet requirement instead of dying

Invasive spread on maturing: 50% chance to claim an adjacent empty plot, 25% chance to overwrite an adjacent germinating seed or dead plant (never an already-grown live neighbor) with a copy of itself

variants.name: Rockwood Glow
variants.unreachable_note: Not obtainable through normal play. Its Temperature/Light requirements can never actually be satisfied on a real farm, no matter how it's set up.
adjacency: Neighbor treated as if lit by UV, regardless of the farm's actual light dial

variants.name: Rockwood Bitter

enrichments.effect: Growth speed / Liquid consumption / Supplement consumption all ×0.8 (-20% each)

adjacency: Neighbor's decay speed if its own requirements go unmet ×0.05 (dies far slower while starved)

crops.note: Woolly Spacekorn fruits into an intermediate shell item (Wooly Korn - 'contains a few spacekorn seed, a grinder is required to extract them') rather than directly back into Spacekorn Seed, unlike the other variants here and unlike any Rockwood Nut variant.

variants.name: Spacekorn Plain
adjacency: +15% Growth/Production speed to neighboring Spacekorn Plain plants specifically (not any neighbor); not consumed on use

Invasive spread on maturing: 50% chance to claim an adjacent empty plot, 25% chance to overwrite an adjacent germinating seed or dead plant (never an already-grown live neighbor) with a copy of itself

variants.name: Spacekorn Sour
adjacency: Neighbor's dead plants auto-dissolve instead of lingering (boolean toggle, any neighbor)

variants.name: Woolly Spacekorn
variants.fruit: Wooly Korn (shell item - grind to extract Spacekorn Seed)

enrichments.effect: Byproduct quantity +100% (stacks with the Neutral Fertilizer entry above if somehow both present)

adjacency: +20% Byproduct quantity to any neighbor; repeatable, not consumed on use
